item.title: 🛢️ First LNG Tanker Breaks Through Hormuz Blockade
item.description: An LNG vessel successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since February, signaling a tentative reopening of the critical waterway. The tanker Mubaraz loaded in the UAE and is now nearing India, though Qatar's LNG capacity remains severely damaged by Iranian missile attacks.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea Executions Doubled During COVID Pandemic
item.description: A new report shows executions and death sentences more than doubled in North Korea after border closures in 2020, with public killings surging for offenses like consuming South Korean media. The regime appears to be intensifying state violence to suppress growing internal discontent.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF Destroys Over 1,000 Hezbollah Sites in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli forces dismantled more than 1,000 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and uncovered weapons caches including anti-tank missiles and mortar shells. The operations come amid escalating tensions with Hezbollah rejecting ceasefire terms.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Calls North Korea Most Pressing Nuclear Challenge
item.description: At the NPT review conference in New York, South Korea's nuclear envoy emphasized North Korea's unique threat to nonproliferation and urged Russia to end military cooperation with Pyongyang. Seoul signaled a shift away from CVID toward a phased denuclearization approach.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia Train Crash Kills 14, Including Women-Only Carriage Victims
item.description: A commuter train collision near Jakarta killed 14 people, mostly women in a women-only carriage, with 84 injured. President Prabowo ordered an investigation and admitted large parts of the rail network are poorly maintained.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran revises school strike death toll to 155, including 120 children
item.description: Iran reported 73 boys and 47 girls killed in a February 28 strike on an elementary school in Minab, down from earlier reports of over 175 dead. A US Tomahawk missile hit the school due to a targeting mistake, according to a preliminary military investigation.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's economy devastated by US-Israel strikes, millions of jobs at risk
item.description: US and Israeli airstrikes damaged 20,000 factories (20% of Iran production). Over 1 million jobs lost directly, with 10-12 million more at risk. Steel and petrochemical sectors crippled. Food prices have surged 68-75% as blockade chokes imports.

item.title: 🇱🇹 Lithuania Charges 13 People in GRU-Linked Assassination Attempts
item.description: Lithuanian authorities charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius tied to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in Russian intelligence operations targeting opponents abroad.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇳🇿 India and New Zealand Sign Major Free Trade Agreement
item.description: India and New Zealand signed a comprehensive free trade agreement that will cut or eliminate tariffs on 95% of goods traded between them, as both nations seek to diversify trade relationships amid global disruptions from the Iran war and US tariff policies.

item.title: 💰⚔️ Global Military Spending Surges to Record $2.9 Trillion
item.description: World military expenditure reached an all-time high of $2.89 trillion in 2025, driven by Europe's rearmament push and rising tensions in Asia, marking the eleventh consecutive year of growth according to SIPRI data.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan PM Launches Defense Policy Review Panel Amid Rising Threats
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi inaugurated a panel to review national security and defense policies as tensions escalate with China, North Korea, and Russia in the Indo-Pacific region.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Rivals Announce Alliance to Challenge Israeli Government
item.description: Two of Benjamin Netanyahu's top rivals announced they would join forces in upcoming elections to challenge his coalition, focusing on domestic issues including military conscription for ultra-Orthodox Israelis.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices climb 1% as Iran war stalemate persists
item.description: Brent crude rose toward $110 per barrel as diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war appeared stalled, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed to shipping.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China denounces Japan and EU over South China Sea at UN
item.description: Beijing accused Tokyo of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and military expansion plans after Japan and the EU raised concerns about South China Sea tensions at a UN Security Council meeting.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over Russian grain shipments
item.description: Kyiv warned Israel about allowing grain shipments from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories, with Foreign Minister Sybiha saying Israel must prevent "stolen" Ukrainian grain from entering its ports.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran proposes reopening Strait of Hormuz while delaying nuclear talks
item.description: Tehran offered to restore maritime traffic through the vital waterway and lift the US blockade, but suggested postponing the contentious nuclear issue negotiations to a later stage.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan holds rates steady in split vote as Iran war fuels inflation concerns
item.description: The BOJ kept its benchmark rate at 0.75% but three of nine board members proposed a hike, signaling growing concern over inflationary pressures from Middle East conflict disrupting energy markets.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy rejects EU associate membership offer, demands full accession by 2027
item.description: Ukraine's president rejected a German-French proposal for associate membership at a Cyprus summit, insisting on full EU membership by 2027. German Chancellor Merz suggested Ukraine may need to accept territorial losses to advance EU prospects.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Moody's Upgrades China Outlook to Stable, Cites Economic Resilience
item.description: Credit ratings agency Moody's revised China's outlook from negative to stable, pointing to resilient economic and fiscal strength despite ongoing trade pressures and geopolitical challenges.

item.title: 🇹🇼 US Pressures Taiwan Parliament to Pass Comprehensive Defense Budget
item.description: The top U.S. diplomat in Taiwan urged the opposition-majority parliament to approve a comprehensive defense budget, emphasizing the critical need for integrated air and missile defense systems amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran War Hands OPEC's Swing Producer Crown to America
item.description: The United States has emerged as the world's dominant energy superpower, stepping in to shield the global economy from the oil crunch triggered by the Iran conflict through increased exports and strategic reserve releases.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸💻 Italy Extradites Chinese Cyber-Espionage Suspect to US
item.description: Italy has extradited Xu Zewei, a 34-year-old alleged Chinese state-sponsored hacker, to the US where he faces charges for stealing COVID-19 vaccine research during the pandemic as part of the "Hafnium" cyber-espionage campaign targeting universities and immunologists.

item.title: 🌍🛢️🌡️ 50+ Nations Launch First-Ever Fossil Fuel Exit Talks in Colombia
item.description: More than 50 governments including major producers like Canada, Norway, Australia and Brazil are meeting in Santa Marta for the inaugural conference on phasing out fossil fuels, bypassing UN climate talks to address the main driver of global warming amid the Iran war energy crunch.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱⚔️ Lebanese Minister Says Hezbollah Disarmament Must Happen
item.description: Lebanon's Social Affairs Minister Haneen Sayed told a CSIS forum in Washington that Hezbollah disarmament "must happen" and is a government demand, but cautioned it would require a political settlement and time given the group's role as a "community" not just a militant organization.

item.title: 🇨🇳🔋💰 CATL Shares Drop 7% After Announcing $5 Billion Renewable Expansion Fund
item.description: World's largest EV battery maker CATL announced a HK$39.2 billion (US$5 billion) private placement to expand its renewables business, with shares falling nearly 7% as investors reacted to the dilution amid global oil price surges from the Iran conflict.
